title: Perianal symptoms GI review

red_flags.text: Supports pain provoked by defecation, pain after defecation, bright-red bleeding, constipation or diarrhoea context, and duration.
red_flags.rationale: The exact fissure presentation section supports defecation-related pain, bleeding, constipation or diarrhoea context, duration, and impact without diagnosing a fissure.
red_flags.evidence_extract: Supports pain provoked by defecation, pain after defecation, bright-red bleeding, constipation or diarrhoea context, and duration.

source.title: The American Society of Colon and Rectal Surgeons Clinical Practice Guidelines for the Management of Anal Fissures

exact_section.heading: Statement of the problem — presentation and symptom pattern

red_flags.text: Supports disease-specific history, bowel habits, bleeding, prolapse, thrombosis, prior interventions, and clinician-performed focused examination.
red_flags.rationale: The exact haemorrhoid evaluation section supports bleeding, prolapse, swelling, discomfort, hygiene, bowel habit, continence, and clinician-performed focused examination without attributing symptoms to haemorrhoids.
red_flags.evidence_extract: Supports disease-specific history, bowel habits, bleeding, prolapse, thrombosis, prior interventions, and clinician-performed focused examination.

source.title: The American Society of Colon and Rectal Surgeons Clinical Practice Guidelines for the Management of Hemorrhoids

treatment_and_management.text: Supports age-appropriate information, multidisciplinary discussion, symptoms, nutritional status, growth, treatment context, and safety-monitoring records.
treatment_and_management.rationale: The exact Crohn section supports recording established IBD, perianal disease, nutrition, treatment context, and existing results without diagnosing Crohn disease.
treatment_and_management.evidence_extract: Supports age-appropriate information, multidisciplinary discussion, symptoms, nutritional status, growth, treatment context, and safety-monitoring records.

referral.text: Supports documentation of clinician-reviewed prior treatment, outcomes, complications, and entered follow-up decisions without creating treatment advice.
referral.rationale: The exact follow-up context supports clinician-entered plan and prior-procedure review without generating treatment, surgery, or referral.
referral.evidence_extract: Supports documentation of clinician-reviewed prior treatment, outcomes, complications, and entered follow-up decisions without creating treatment advice.

source_limitations: Pain, bleeding, discharge, swelling, fever, bowel context, consent, chaperone, examination, diagnosis, imaging, medicine, procedure, referral, and urgency remain unsupported.
Any legacy item not explicitly mapped to an exact section remains unsupported pending qualified clinician review.
No mapping authorises an automatic diagnosis, differential, investigation, management decision, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, urgency conclusion, discharge decision, or patient instruction.
All prior supported legacy-item mappings were removed because canonical item-level applicability and workflow-specific rationale were not present in explicit repository records.
curation_policy: Direct guideline curation: retain only explicitly mapped content; rewrite only with explicit item mapping; remove unmapped legacy content; add only committed exact-section evidence summaries.
